agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"payment-notification-service","to":"database","status":"correct","issue":"Health check includes database dependency verification"},{"from":"payment-notification-service","to":"message-broker","status":"correct","issue":"Retry with exponential backoff (max 3) handles transient publish failures"},{"from":"payment-notification-service","to":"email-provider","status":"correct","issue":"Circuit breaker with 30s reset protects against downstream email outages"},{"from":"payment-notification-service","to":"all-outbound-http","status":"correct","issue":"5s timeout prevents thread/connection pool exhaustion from hung dependencies"}],"contract_compliance":"Health endpoint covers both primary dependencies (database, message broker). Structured logging includes request ID for correlation. No contract gaps identified in described scope.","data_flow_integrity":"Request flow is traceable via request ID in structured logs. Message broker retry logic preserves at-least-once delivery semantics up to 3 attempts. After 3 failures, message fate is unspecified — see failure modes."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"adequate","gaps":["Confirm circuit breaker state transitions (open/half-open/closed) are logged so on-call can distinguish 'email provider down' from 'our service broken'","Confirm retry attempt count is logged per request so you can spot broker degradation before full failure"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"Strong baseline: request rate, error rate, p99 latency via Prometheus","gaps":["Add circuit breaker state metric (0/1 gauge) for alerting when email provider trips","Add retry count histogram to detect broker degradation trend","Add message broker publish success/failure counters separate from HTTP error rate","Add in-flight request gauge to detect connection pool saturation"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"Request ID present in logs enables basic correlation","gaps":["No mention of distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry/Jaeger). For a payment-adjacent service, being able to trace a notification back to the originating payment event across services is high value. Nice-to-have for launch, should-have for month one."]},"debugging_capability":"Good for single-service debugging: structured JSON logs with request ID, duration, status, and error details give an on-call engineer what they need to triage. Cross-service correlation depends on whether upstream callers propagate the same request ID."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"Message broker publish fails after 3 retries","handling":"partial","recommendation":"MUST-HAVE: Define what happens after retry exhaustion. Dead-letter queue? Log-and-drop? Alert? A payment notification silently lost is a customer-facing incident. Add a DLQ or persistent fallback and alert on retry exhaustion."},{"scenario":"Email provider circuit breaker opens","handling":"handled","recommendation":"Circuit breaker is in place. Verify notifications are queued/retried after circuit closes (half-open state), not dropped. If they are dropped, add a redelivery mechanism."},{"scenario":"Database becomes unavailable","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Health check will report unhealthy, but confirm the service returns 503 to callers and load balancer removes it from rotation, rather than accepting requests it cannot process."},{"scenario":"Upstream sends burst of notifications (spike load)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"NICE-TO-HAVE: No mention of rate limiting or backpressure. If upstream payment system retries or replays events, this service could amplify into duplicate notifications. Consider idempotency keys and/or rate limiting."},{"scenario":"Poison message in broker causes repeated processing failure","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"SHOULD-HAVE: Without a DLQ or skip mechanism, a poison message could block the consumer. Add max-delivery-count with DLQ routing."}],"retry_config":"Exponential backoff, max 3 retries on broker publish. Appropriate for transient failures. Confirm jitter is included to avoid thundering herd on broker recovery.","timeout_config":"5s on all outbound HTTP. Reasonable default. Verify this applies to connection establishment AND read separately — a 5s connect timeout is too generous; consider 1-2s connect, 5s read.","circuit_breakers":"30s reset on email provider. Reasonable for a notification use case. Confirm failure threshold (how many failures trip it) and success threshold (how many successes close it) are tuned, not just defaults.","graceful_degradation":"Circuit breaker provides graceful degradation for email. No mention of graceful shutdown (drain in-flight requests on SIGTERM before container stops). Confirm graceful shutdown is implemented for zero-downtime deploys."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":true,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Redeploy previous container image. No schema migrations in this release, so rollback is clean with no data compatibility concerns.","monitoring_coverage":"Prometheus metrics cover the three golden signals (rate, errors, latency). Gaps exist in component-level metrics (circuit breaker state, retry counts, broker publish counters). Alerting rules not described — must confirm alerts exist for: error rate spike, p99 latency breach, health check failure, circuit breaker open.","on_call_documentation":"Structured logging with request ID enables triage. Recommend a runbook covering: (1) what to do when circuit breaker trips, (2) how to inspect/replay DLQ messages, (3) how to verify broker connectivity, (4) rollback procedure step-by-step.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":["Dead-letter queue for exhausted retries — critical gap"],"config_changes":[],"infra_provisioning":[]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":true,"data_preserving":true,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"No schema migrations in this release. Rollback is a clean image swap."},"verdict":"READY WITH CAVEATS"},"escalations":[{"type":"integration_gap","issue":"No dead-letter queue or fallback for messages that exhaust all retries","reason":"After 3 retry failures on broker publish, the notification's fate is undefined. For a payment notification service, silently losing a notification is a customer-facing incident. A DLQ or persistent fallback with alerting is required before this can be fully production-ready.","routed_to":"MJ"}],"confidence":{"level":65,"high_confidence_areas":["Rollback safety — no migrations, clean image swap","Basic observability — structured logging and Prometheus metrics are solid","Timeout and circuit breaker configuration — appropriate for the use case"],"low_confidence_areas":["Post-retry-exhaustion behavior — not specified, could mean silent message loss","Alerting rules — metrics exist but no confirmation alerts are configured","Graceful shutdown — not mentioned, risk of dropped requests during deploys","Distributed tracing — absent, limits cross-service debugging for payment flows"],"assumptions":["Structured logs are shipped to a centralized log aggregator (ELK/Loki/etc)","Prometheus metrics are scraped and connected to an alerting system (Grafana/PagerDuty)","Container orchestration (K8s or equivalent) uses the /health endpoint for liveness/readiness probes","Exponential backoff includes jitter"]}}
